name: Clack (@clack/prompts — Modern CLI Prompts)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Nate Moore / Astro team

performance.notes: Waits for user input; no computational overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Modern CLI prompts with beautiful UX. Primitives: intro(), outro(), text(), confirm(), select(), multiselect(), spinner(), note(), cancel(). Opinionated design with emoji indicators and clean visual style. Async/await API. TypeScript-first. Used by create-astro, create-solid, and modern framework CLIs. Superior visual output vs Inquirer.js. isCancel() handles Ctrl+C gracefully. group() for sequential prompts. @clack/core for unstyled building blocks. Nate Moore created it for the Astro CLI. Becoming the new standard for modern TypeScript CLIs.

gotchas: Opinionated visual style — cannot easily customize appearance
Fewer prompt types than Inquirer (no editor, number built-in)
Non-interactive CI environments need isCancel() checks
best_when: Modern TypeScript CLIs wanting polished UX out of the box
Scaffolding tools for frameworks (create-*, init commands)
Teams preferring aesthetic CLI output over Inquirer's plain style
New projects where Inquirer's legacy API is undesirable
avoid_when: When extensive prompt type variety needed (Inquirer broader)
Legacy Node.js codebases already using Inquirer deeply
summary: Clack (@clack/prompts) is the modern alternative to Inquirer.js with superior visual UX. intro/outro/text/confirm/select/multiselect/spinner/note primitives with beautiful styling. Used by create-astro, create-solid. TypeScript-first async/await API. Becoming the new standard for modern TypeScript scaffolding CLIs. Cleaner API and better aesthetics than Inquirer, with @clack/core for unstyled building blocks.
